title: Domain Escalation
module_title: Active Directory Attacks and Domain Privilege Escalation
learning_objectives: Enumerate Active Directory structure and trust relationships
Execute Kerberoasting to extract and crack service tickets
Perform AS-REP roasting on pre-authentication disabled accounts
Perform credential relay and reuse attacks
Escalate from domain user to domain admin
scenario: Foothold on a domain-joined workstation. Escalate from domain user to domain admin via AD authentication attacks.

scenario_context.setting: Domain-joined workstation with valid user credentials
scenario_context.challenge: Escalate from standard domain user to domain admin
scenario_context.relevance: AD exploitation is critical for enterprise network compromise

subtasks.title: Domain Enumeration
subtasks.description: Users, groups, SPNs, computer accounts, trusts.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use PowerShell, Impacket, and Bloodhound to enumerate domain structure. Document users, groups, SPNs, computer accounts, and domain trusts.
subtasks.evidence_required: Domain enumeration with attack findings identified

subtasks.title: Kerberoasting
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Enumerate SPNs with GetUserSPNs.py. Request Kerberos tickets for service accounts. Crack extracted tickets. Document successful cracks.
subtasks.evidence_required: Kerberoast log: SPN, ticket hash, cracked password

subtasks.title: AS-REP Roasting
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Find accounts with pre-authentication disabled. Extract AS-REP hashes. Crack hashes. Document accounts compromised.

subtasks.title: Credential Relay/Reuse
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test discovered credentials on other systems. Document credential reuse across domain. Perform lateral movement.
subtasks.evidence_required: Credential reuse matrix with lateral movement proof

subtasks.title: Domain Admin Achievement
subtasks.description: Achieve DA through any valid chain.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use any of the above attacks to escalate to domain admin. Document the complete exploitation chain.
subtasks.evidence_required: Domain admin access evidence + chain documentation

subtasks.title: AD Security Assessment
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Write assessment covering enumeration findings, vulnerabilities exploited, attack paths used, and recommendations.

reflection_prompt: What AD security weaknesses did you discover? How would hardening help mitigate these attacks?

quiz.question: Why is Kerberoasting effective against AD service accounts?
options: Anyone can request service tickets using SPN queries
quiz.explanation: Any authenticated domain user can request Kerberos tickets for services. If the service account password is weak, the ticket can be cracked offline.

quiz.question: What accounts are vulnerable to AS-REP roasting?
quiz.explanation: AS-REP roasting targets accounts with pre-authentication disabled, allowing attackers to extract crackable hashes without valid credentials.

portfolio_artifacts: Domain enumeration with identified attack vectors
Kerberoast log with service accounts and results
AS-REP roasting results with cracked accounts
Domain admin access evidence with chain documentation
mentor_notes: Domain admin achievement is mandatory. Assessment should show AD understanding and attack chain clarity. 72-hour deadline allows thorough testing. Recommendations should be actionable.
